task_description: Reproduce or summarise the empirical distribution of interestingness scores reported in the paper, including discussion of the Zipfian frequency distribution of phrases in the corpus.
adaptation_notes: LLM helped structure the analysis of the frequency-rank distribution and suggested using log-log plots to verify power-law behaviour. Final code was independently written and verified.
top_5_prompts: How does the Zipfian distribution of phrase frequencies in Twitter relate to the choice of power-law exponent in the interestingness formula?
Write Python code to plot the frequency-rank distribution of phrases in a simulated corpus on a log-log scale and annotate the slope.
What is the relationship between corpus-level frequency distributions and the signal-to-noise ratio of the interestingness score?
How does normalising by total frequency prevent high-frequency phrases from dominating trend rankings?
Reproduce Figure 1 from Goorha & Ungar (2010): what axes does it show and what visual pattern is key to interpreting the results?
